creator: rakuco@FreeBSD.org
text:
dinoex@FreeBSD.org writes:

> The error message is missing
>
> FreeBSD 11.0-CURRENT is not supported.

Please re-read what I wrote.

I have linked to ports/185067 and explained this is supposed to fix that
error. I've also explained in that PR what causes the error. That should
also be enough to show that there is nothing 11-specific here, and it
should be clear from both pkg-fallout and portsmon that this port has
been failing on 10 and 11 for ages.

> disabeling the tests on all versions is not a solution.

Then please require libstdc++, disable the tests only when libc++ or
certain FreeBSD versions are used, mark the port as BROKEN on certain
versions or fix the gtest code to build with libc++, just letting the
port stay broken indefinitely is the worst option.
